page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 33.6 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 13.85 GB/sec. Geekbench testing shows strong performance with a single-core score of 2074.0 and a multi-core score of 15951.0, alongside strong image and text processing metrics. Static web serving throughput is also highly competitive, reaching 51.12 GB/sec.

For memory and compute-intensive tasks, the Intel Xeon processor utilizes advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning and cryptography acceleration. Memory bandwidth tests show cached read speeds of 2625.73 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 162.39 GB/sec. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed DDR5 memory but do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.

This server is well-suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, software compilation, and moderate-sized machine learning inference. It demonstrates solid performance in small-model LLM text generation, delivering 286.26 tokens/sec for a 135M parameter model, making it a viable option for lightweight AI inference, web application hosting, and general compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, delivering 2.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in single-core and multi-core operations, particularly in gzip compression, Geekbench image and text processing, and static web serving. It also supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated matrix and cryptographic operations. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, ideal for high-performance web serving and batch processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86 server featuring DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix extensions for high-throughput processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions
Delivers strong performance in web serving and single-threaded compression
Features 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for software compilation, batch processing, and lightweight inference
